creator: sunpoet@FreeBSD.org
text:
We could add stripping to:
(1) an universal solution (e.g. Mk/Uses/strip.mk)
(2) various (bsd.)foo-lang.mk
(3) individual ports

I agree that (1) is better than others. But before that, I would suggest to finish stripping in (2) and cleanup codes in (3).

In this case, exp-run cannot help to find runtime error. But if we really encounter such error, we could add NO_STRIP=yes (or some other knob) to keep it unchanged.
